data.title: Haidilao's Overseas Boss Returns To China To Reignite Slowing Growth
data.description:
The return of 31-year company veteran Yang Lijuan, who oversaw the hotpot chain's global operation, may be its best hope for reviving stagnating revenues and declining profits at home

image credit: Bamboo Works

Key Takeaways:

  • Haidilao announced that veteran Yang Lijuan, CEO of its overseas business, will return to the parent company, sparking a 7% jump in its shares
  • The former hotpot giant's profit fell in 2025 after a big drop in customer traffic, despite shedding more than 11,000 employees

Haidilao's shares jumped by nearly 7% the day after the announcement last week and have held on to most of the gains since then, showing investors are hopeful that Zhang can breathe new life into the company.

Yang certainly has the credentials that could make her the right person for the task. She was previously CEO of Haidilao in 2022, when she was tasked with closing underperforming restaurants after an aggressive expansion and the pandemic caused the company to stumble. Within a year, Yang returned Haidilao from a 3.2 billion yuan ($469.3 million) loss in 2021 back to a profit of 1.6 billion in 2022, earning her the "firefighter" moniker.

Two years later, in June 2024, Yang took over at the helm of the company's global unit, Super Hi International Holding Ltd. (9658.HK, HDL.US). That unit now trades at a price to earnings (P/E) ratio of 24, higher than that the 18 for Haidilao, in classic case of an offspring outperforming its older parent. Her return comes after Super Hi reported 8% revenue growth last year as the company's profit rose 70%.

High table turnover

At its peak in 2018, Haidilao was able to achieve impressive table turnover of five times a day, with 60% growth in both revenue and profits for the year. It became legendary for turning hotpots into a dining experience rather than just a meal, offering customers free manicures, shoeshines, snacks and "noodle dances" where waiters roamed restaurants hand-pulling fresh noodles at individual tables.

But much has changed since then. In its most recent annual results, table turnover fell to 3.9 times a day last year from 4.1 times in 2024, far below the rate in 2018. Haidilao's 2025 revenue of 43.2 billion yuan ($6.3 billion) was just 1% above 2024 and its profit fell 14% to 4 billion yuan. The weak showing was partly the result of falling customer visits to its self-operated Haidilao restaurants, which dropped by 7.5% for the year to 383.9 million.

Haidilao has tried to steady its ship by downsizing both its workforce and trimming the number of its self-operated restaurants, which typically have lower margins than franchised outlets. It laid off 11,558 employees in 2025, after cutting an even larger 16,569 in 2024. It shut down 85 low-performing self-operated restaurants, and opened 79, ending the year with 1,304 self-operated and 79 franchised restaurants.

It is staking its future on developing the higher margin franchise business as well as take-out services and new brands. These have a long way to go, however. The franchise business contributed just 0.6% of revenues in 2025, or 270 million yuan, although that was up from a negligible contribution in 2024. Haidilao's take-out business contributed 2.65 billion yuan in revenue last year, more than double 1.25 billion in 2024.

New restaurant brands contributed 1.52 billion yuan in 2025, more than triple the 483.3 million yuan in 2024, though it still made up just 3.5% of the company's revenue pie. A food ingredients business is also expanding, serving growing appetite from consumers making their own hotpots at home. That business generated 1.1 billion yuan in 2025, nearly double the 575.1 million yuan in 2024, but, again, accounting for just 2.7% of revenue last year.

Competitive market

Haidilao's new revenue streams may be critical for its survival. China's hotpot market is still growing, serving as a sort of comfort food and also popular for its social elements of group dining. But it's showing signs of slowing after years of explosive growth.

According to Hongcan Big Data, the national hotpot market was worth 639 billion yuan in 2025, up 3.5% over 2024, and is projected to rise to 670 billion yuan this year. But even as the market expanded in revenue terms last year, the number of hot pot restaurants fell by 15.3% to 448,000, according to the same source.

Haidilao isn't the only former restaurant superstar suffering, as customers constantly seek out new experiences away from the big brands. Among its peers, personal hotpot specialist Xiabuxiabu (0520.HK) has seen declining revenue and losses, while Jiumaojiu (9922.HK), known for its pots of "sauerkraut fish," saw its revenue fall and profit flatline last year.

The generation gap was illustrated earlier this year when an employee whistleblower complained that Haidilao was forcing him and others to buy "apology" gifts worth 500 yuan out of their own pockets when customers made complaints. The issue created a national scandal on social media, and Zhang later made a public apology and pledged to increase his stake in the company.

While Haidilao conducted an investigation and refunded some money to four employees, the story caused major reputational damage for the company, which positions itself as a purveyor of fun and family values.

That reputational damage is just one of many challenges now confronting a returning "firefighter" Yang, showing she has her work cut out for her.

To subscribe to Bamboo Works free weekly newsletter, click here

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Google's Enterprise AI Push Gains Teeth, Analysts See Cloud Upside
data.description:
Analyst Sees Upside From AI And Cloud Positioning

Jones said on Thursday that he is "incrementally positive" on Google's role in agentic AI, citing its vertically integrated cloud offering.

He noted that Google's ability to combine its own chips with third-party Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) and integrate across platforms should help drive higher enterprise cloud and AI spending.

BNP Paribas set a $390 price forecast on the stock, implying about 15% upside from the $339.3 level.

Cloud Revenue Growth Could Accelerate

Jones said Google's shift from AI proof-of-concept to enabling enterprises to build and scale agents could boost cloud revenue growth in the medium term.

He added that easier data access and multi-cloud capabilities reduce friction for customers, which should support increased budgets for AI adoption.

Capex And Infrastructure Support Long-Term Strategy

Jones said Google's rising capital expenditure remains "reasonable," given its focus on scaling agentic AI.

He pointed to new AI infrastructure, including advanced Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) and data architecture, as key enablers of long-term growth as enterprise demand for AI continues to expand.

Technical Analysis

Alphabet is pressing the upper end of its 52-week range, sitting just below the $349.00 high, which is consistent with buyers staying in control. The stock is trading 7.8% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 7.5% above its 100-day SMA, suggesting the short- and intermediate-term trends remain higher.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum indicator, is bullish with the MACD line above the signal line and the histogram positive, which suggests strengthening upside momentum. In everyday terms, MACD staying above its signal line usually means pullbacks are bought faster than rallies fade.

Over the last 12 months, the stock has been up 112.76%, which shows the longer-term tape has rewarded staying with the uptrend. With the price now near the $340.50 resistance area, the next test is whether buyers can absorb supply that has recently stalled rallies.

  • Key Resistance: $340.50 — a spot where upside attempts have tended to stall.
  • Key Support: $294.00 — an area where demand has previously shown up on pullbacks.

Earnings Outlook

The countdown is on: Alphabet is set to report earnings on April 29, 2026 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: $2.62 (Down from $2.81 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $104.04 Billion (Up from $90.23 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 31.3x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because GOOGL carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

GOOGL Stock Price Activity: Alphabet shares were up 0.06% at $339.10 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Why Tesla's Latest Earnings Don't Justify Its Massive Valuation
data.description:
Musk Defends Higher Spending As Growth Investment

Elon Musk told CNBC on Thursday that Tesla will “substantially” increase capital expenditures to support future growth. He argued the spending is “well-justified for a substantially increased future revenue stream.”

His comments highlight Tesla’s focus on long-term opportunities, even as near-term financial pressures build.

Wells Fargo Flags Weak Fundamentals And Limited Catalysts

Wells Fargo analyst Colin Langan told CNBC that Tesla’s core fundamentals remain “very tough.” He noted that about 70% of the earnings beat came from one-time items and should not be extrapolated.

“Well, I think you have a combination here of the fundamentals. I mean, the quarter, if you take out, you know, some of the one-time items, wasn’t really enough to justify a 200 times earnings valuation,” Langan said.

He added that rising capex is not matched by near-term product momentum, with delays in programs like the Semi and Optimus limiting excitement for 2025.

Robotaxi Concerns Add Execution Risk

Langan said robotaxis—seen as Tesla’s key growth driver—”won’t be material to revenue until next year,” reducing near-term upside.

He also pointed to operational concerns, including reported accidents and limited deployment scale. He argued that the company lacks a clear catalyst.

Langan added that many autonomy experts question Tesla’s vision-only approach, calling it high in execution risk, which reinforces his bearish stance.

TSLA Price Action: Tesla shares were up 0.53% at $375.70 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Stock Hits 52-Week High - Here's Why
data.description:
Regulatory Shift Fuels Buying Momentum

Taiwan's Financial Supervisory Commission said it plans to raise the cap on single-stock holdings for domestic equity funds and active ETFs focused on local stocks from 10% to 25%.

The previous limit had restricted fund managers from increasing exposure to Taiwan Semiconductor despite the AI-driven rally, Bloomberg reported on Friday.

Analysts See Strong Inflows And Market Impact

JPMorgan told Bloomberg that the policy change could attract more than $6 billion in inflows and help lift the Taiex Index further.

The bank also upgraded Taiwan to Overweight and raised its targets for the benchmark TAIEX index, citing improving hardware pricing and easing monetization concerns.

Concentration Risks And Valuation Effects

While the move boosts demand for Taiwan Semiconductor, analysts flagged higher concentration risk given the company's large index weight.

Union Bancaire Privée's Vey-Sern Ling told Bloomberg that the higher cap means "these domestic funds will buy more Taiwan Semiconductor," which could also narrow the valuation gap between its Taiwan-listed shares and U.S. ADRs.

Technical Analysis

TSM is pressing into the top of its 52-week range, consistent with buyers remaining in control of the longer-term trend. The stock is trading 11% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 17.8% above its 100-day SMA, a setup that leans toward sustained upside pressure across short- and intermediate-term timeframes.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum indicator, is bullish with the MACD line above the signal line, suggesting momentum still favors the upside. When MACD holds in that posture, pullbacks often act more like pauses than full trend breaks.

The stock's 133.07% gain over the past 12 months shows how powerful the longer-term uptrend has been, even with periodic shakeouts. With the price now above the prior 52-week high, traders typically watch whether breakouts hold or quickly fade back into the old range.

  • Key Resistance: N/A — no clearly defined overhead level is flagged in the current setup.
  • Key Support: $360.50 — an area where buyers have tended to show up on dips.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the July 16, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $3.67 (Up from $2.47 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $39.61 Billion (Up from $30.07 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 32.8x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $420.00. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Barclays: Overweight (Raises Target to $470.00) (April 22)
  • DA Davidson: Buy (Maintains Target to $450.00) (April 17)
  • Needham: Buy (Raises Target to $480.00) (April 16)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because TSM carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

TSM Price Action: Taiwan Semiconductor shares were up 3.32% at $395.36 during premarket trading on Friday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week high, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock
